summary: Matched additional Yoshi special-move routines in ftYs_SpecialN.c, replacing three nonmatching stubs with simple C implementations and refining ftYoshiAttributes layout so the new code could access dat_attrs fields around 0x114. The PR also removed some extra blank lines. Evidence is limited to the diff excerpt; no review comments or explanatory PR body were present.
what_changed: Implemented ftYs_SpecialN2_0_Coll by calling ft_8008403C with the Yoshi fighter object cast to Fighter_GObj* and callback fn_8012D428.
Implemented ftYs_SpecialAirLw_Anim: obtains Fighter* with GET_FIGHTER, checks ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, and sets fp->cmd_vars[1] to 1 when animation frames are exhausted.

Implemented ftYs_SpecialAirLw_Phys: obtains Fighter* and ftYoshiAttributes* from fp->dat_attrs, calls ft_80085134, then clamps fp->self_vel.y to attributes->x114 when velocity is below that value or cmd_vars[1] is nonzero.
Refined ftYoshiAttributes in types.h by changing data_filler1 size from 0xD7 to 0xCA and adding float fields x114, x118, and x11C before x120.
Annotated ftYoshiAttributes as corresponding to x2D4 / fp->dat_attrs.
Removed a comment saying the struct additions were only to match ftYs_SpecialN_8012CDB4 and removed several extra blank lines in ftYs_SpecialN.c.
smart_moves: Used existing helper callbacks and patterns instead of hand-expanding collision logic: ftYs_SpecialN2_0_Coll mirrors nearby collision wrappers by forwarding to ft_8008403C with a different callback.
Matched an airborne down-special physics routine by exposing the needed Yoshi attribute offset as a named struct field rather than continuing to rely on opaque filler.
Kept newly introduced attribute fields unnamed by offset-style names, x114/x118/x11C, which is appropriate when semantic names are not yet evidenced.

Preserved known fighter API usage patterns: GET_FIGHTER for Fighter access, fp->dat_attrs for character attributes, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining for animation-end checks, and cmd_vars for script/animation state communication.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter move routines, compare with adjacent ground/air variants: small wrappers often differ only in helper choice or callback argument.
Attribute-struct filler should be split only as far as required by observed accesses; adding offset-based fields can unlock matches while avoiding unsupported semantic names.
Animation routines may signal later physics behavior through fp->cmd_vars; preserving this state handoff can be necessary for matching related move phases.
For Yoshi dat_attrs, fields around offsets 0x114, 0x118, and 0x11C were exposed in ftYoshiAttributes during work on SpecialN/SpecialLw routines.
A small dummy stack declaration, shown as u8 _[8], may be used as a matching tactic when a function requires stack-shape adjustment, though the diff does not provide the reason beyond matching.
naming_conventions: Yoshi move functions use the ftYs_ prefix and action-state-style names such as ftYs_SpecialN2_0_Coll, ftYs_SpecialAirLw_Anim, and ftYs_SpecialAirLw_Phys.
Unidentified character attributes are named by hexadecimal-like offset fields, for example x114, x118, x11C, and x120.
The Yoshi attributes typedef remains ftYoshiAttributes, with the underlying tag _ftYoshiAttributes.
Opaque fighter command variables are referenced as fp->cmd_vars[index] until clearer semantics are known.
Callback/helper symbols without known names retain generated names such as fn_8012D428 and ft_8008403C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced nonmatching markers, /// #ftYs_SpecialN2_0_Coll, /// #ftYs_SpecialAirLw_Anim, and /// #ftYs_SpecialAirLw_Phys, with C implementations.
Used explicit casts from ftYs_GObj* or HSD_GObj* to Fighter_GObj* for calls into shared fighter helpers.
Inserted u8 _[8] in ftYs_SpecialAirLw_Phys, likely to influence stack layout for matching; the diff provides no additional explanation.
Compared cmd_vars[1] as ((u32) fp->cmd_vars[1] != 0U), indicating attention to exact signedness/boolean codegen.
Adjusted ftYoshiAttributes filler length so x114 lands at the needed offset before x120.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftYoshiAttributes fields x114, x118, or x11C with semantic names.
Search for other Yoshi SpecialLw implementations or action-state variants that use cmd_vars[1] and self_vel.y clamping.
Check whether the u8 _[8] stack-padding tactic in ftYs_SpecialAirLw_Phys was later removed or replaced by a cleaner match.
Compare ftYs_SpecialN2_0_Coll and ftYs_SpecialN2_1_Coll to confirm the callback distinction between fn_8012D428 and fn_8012D3C0.
